title: Remove need for _USE_MATH_DEFINES
author: ePirat
body: Once we can break API/ABI, remove/encapsulate things accordingly to not need to expose this define to library users.

title: Actually handle failure to load SOFA file
author: ePirat
body:
Currently when a SOFA file fails to load, there is only an error message printed but the caller is not informed of the failure in any way, which makes it impossible to know programmatically that something went wrong.

https://github.com/videolan/libspatialaudio/blob/7946d989f1f79a3cfbd7545dd92fdc34ee9ec9e6/source/hrtf/sofa_hrtf.cpp#L23

When the caller continues with the processing as it does not know about the error, the program crashes or aborts with the following assertion:

> Assertion failed: (gains.size() == m_nCh), function calculateGains, file DirectSpeakersGainCalc.cpp, line 112.

title: Renderer: Silence rendered for DirectSpeakers without audioPackFormatID
author: ePirat
body:
Using the Renderer class to render DirectSpeaker channels, I only get silence as output when not providing an
`audioPackFormatID`.

title: Build: Do not build test executables by default
author: ePirat
body: We should not build the test executable by default. Need to check if thats possible with CMake, if not, at least we should have an option to disable testing altogether to avoid compiling things that are never needed.

title: [RFC] Migration to VideoLAN
author: jbkempf
body:
Hello,

It's very likely that I will move this repository to a VideoLAN full project, since there are almost no paying customers, and this is only used by VLC and FFmpeg heavily.

Ping @fkuehne @peterStitt @ePirat 

Remarks?

title: Support for arbitrary speaker layouts
author: jcelerier
body:
Hello!

The documentation here mentions only a very specific and narrow set of supported speaker configurations:
https://github.com/videolabs/libspatialaudio/blob/master/docs/RendererOverview.md

Is there a way to set-up the renderer with custom immersive loudspeaker configurations?

For instance at the [SAT ](https://sat.qc.ca/) there's a full-dome setup (works fine with VBAP) + an immersive square black box for which we mainly use SpatGRIS's MBAP (https://gris.musique.umontreal.ca/). DBAP is also an option.

If it's not the case, would you be interested in a PR to add support for a renderer for custom configurations?

Thanks for this library and happy holidays!

title: Improve documentation about limitations of MIT HRTF
author: ePirat
body: It should be clearly documented that with the built-in MIT HRTF the binaural rendering won't work for higher order ambisonics.

title: Remove kiss_fft from public headers
author: ePirat
body:
Currently kiss_fft headers are installed, thats not as trivial to change as some classes use `kiss_fft_cpx` and `kiss_fftr_cfg` types in their class private members, so without the kiss_fft headers the public headers for these classes would cause errors when included by API users.

The classes are:
- AmbisonicBinauralizer
- AmbisonicShelfFilters
- Decorrelator

The most trivial way to solve this seems to be the PIMPL idiom, see https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/language/pimpl.html
It would also mean that adding or removing private members would no longer break ABI, making it a lot easier to maintain stable ABI in the future.

title: Add version defines to the header
author: ePirat
body: Given the recent API changes, we need to have a way to differentiate at compile time between the different spatialaudio versions so that downstream consumers like VLC can properly adjust the APIs to be compatible and have a way to tell if the new APIs are actually available.

title: Properly mark exported symbols
author: ePirat
body: Right now we do not mark any public symbols as exported leading to the Windows shared library being unusable, as uncovered by #54

title: Please release a new version
author: sebastinas
body: It's been 8 years since the last release and there are enough changes on master for a new release. Please prepare a new release.

title: Bogus Zlib dependency?
author: JohannesKauffmann
body:
[`spatialaudio.pc.cmake`](https://github.com/videolabs/libspatialaudio/blob/6e158835a96df9d89aacea54b4792a8d092e34e4/cmake/spatialaudio.pc.cmake#L8) lists Zlib as a dependency, but AFAICT Zlib isn't used throughout the library. Maybe libmysofa depends on it? In any case, linking to spatialaudio 0.3.0 as shipped with Ubuntu 23.04 in a VLC build _without_ linking to `-lz` seemed to work fine.

I'm noticing this now as I didn't have `zlib1g-dev` installed, but the linker complained about missing `-lz` ...

title: API break with commit  [Fixed a bug that caused an allocation error]
author: kwizart
body:
[Fixed a bug that caused an allocation error](https://github.com/videolabs/libspatialaudio/pull/24/commits/10e70839a9ad019e455c48c9d2bf93016ba37633)

This commit breaks API with vlc-3.x
Is there a way to avoid the API break ?

```
audio_filter/channel_mixer/spatialaudio.cpp: In function 'int Open(vlc_object_t*)':
audio_filter/channel_mixer/spatialaudio.cpp:512:33: error: no matching function for call to 'CAmbisonicZoomer::Configure(unsigned int&, bool, int)'
  512 |     if (!p_sys->zoomer.Configure(p_sys->i_order, true, 0))
      |          ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
In file included from /usr/include/spatialaudio/AmbisonicProcessor.h:22,
                 from /usr/include/spatialaudio/Ambisonics.h:28,
                 from audio_filter/channel_mixer/spatialaudio.cpp:43:
/usr/include/spatialaudio/AmbisonicZoomer.h:40:18: note: candidate: 'virtual bool CAmbisonicZoomer::Configure(unsigned int, bool, unsigned int, unsigned int)'
   40 |     virtual bool Configure(unsigned nOrder, bool b3D, unsigned nBlockSize, unsigned nMisc);
      |                  ^~~~~~~~~
/usr/include/spatialaudio/AmbisonicZoomer.h:40:18: note:   candidate expects 4 arguments, 3 provided
make[4]: *** [Makefile:26640: audio_filter/channel_mixer/libspatialaudio_plugin_la-spatialaudio.lo] Error 1
```

_Originally posted by @kwizart in https://github.com/videolabs/libspatialaudio/issues/24#issuecomment-1453107143_

title: SIGSEGV when running the example provided in the Readme
author: jrialland

title: Cannot compile simple code
author: mramalingam2203
body:
I installed the library via ubuntu packages and it seems the header files need to be included in the compilation:
`g++ code.cpp -I/home/muthu/Documents/work/kickback/libspatialaudio/include  -I/home/muthu/Documents/work/kickback/libspatialaudio/include/hrtf  -I/home/muthu/Documents/work/kickback/libspatialaudio/source/kiss_fft -lspatialaudio`

While some of the functions of the object could be linked some could not be. The error is like this:
_/usr/bin/ld: /tmp/ccQTjiik.o: in function `main':
code.cpp:(.text+0x115): undefined reference to `CAmbisonicEncoder::SetPosition(PolarPoint, float)'
/usr/bin/ld: code.cpp:(.text+0x182): undefined reference to `CAmbisonicDecoder::Configure(unsigned int, bool, unsigned int, int, unsigned int)'_

May I know how it can be resolved?

collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status

title: Debug logs always printed
author: sergiogf93
body:
There are some uses of `std::cout` that result on log lines always being printed. For example these two:

https://github.com/videolabs/libspatialaudio/blob/master/source/AmbisonicBinauralizer.cpp#L362
https://github.com/videolabs/libspatialaudio/blob/master/source/AmbisonicBinauralizer.cpp#L367

Would be nice to have some setting to only print these in debug mode.

title: Library is built twice
author: sergiogf93
body:
When using this library as a dependency called from a higher CMakeLists, when compiling with ninja an error is raised because the library is built both as a shared and a static library:

https://github.com/videolabs/libspatialaudio/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt#L15

Is there a way to avoid this and have it only built as shared for example?

title: Dynamic position changes and partial encoding?
author: TomArrow
body: For 3d rendering purposes in a game engine I was wondering if this could be of use. The challenge is that the position of the audio sources changes on each frame and that audio gets only rendered in small chunks. Is there any way to get a nice continuous audio from that using this library or will I end up with clicks/pops?

title: AmbisonicProcessor: Rotating around {0, 0, 0} is not a null op in 3rd order
author: konradglas
